name: wls-wsat XMLDecoder RCE
description: Vulnerability in the Oracle WebLogic Server wls-wsat component allows unauthenticated remote code execution via XMLDecoder deserialization.
recommendation: Apply Oracle CPU October 2017 or upgrade WebLogic.

name: wls9-async Deserialization RCE
description: Remote code execution via deserialization in the wls9_async and wls-wsat components of Oracle WebLogic Server.
recommendation: Apply Oracle out-of-band patch for CVE-2019-2725.

name: Unauthenticated Console Takeover
description: An easily exploitable vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to take over the WebLogic Administration Console via HTTP.

name: Console Post-Authentication RCE
description: After gaining console access (e.g., via CVE-2020-14882), an attacker can leverage this vulnerability to execute arbitrary commands on the server.

name: Console Takeover bypass variant
description: Bypass of CVE-2020-14882 fix allows unauthenticated attackers to access the WebLogic Console via alternative URL encoding.
recommendation: Apply Oracle out-of-band patch for CVE-2020-14750.

name: Coherence T3 Deserialization RCE
description: A vulnerability in Oracle Coherence product via T3 protocol allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code.

name: Coherence T3 Deserialization bypass
description: A second Coherence gadget chain bypasses the fix for CVE-2020-2555, allowing unauthenticated RCE via T3.

name: IIOP JNDI Injection RCE
description: An IIOP protocol vulnerability allows JNDI injection leading to remote code execution with network access via IIOP.

name: T3 Deserialization bypass via JDK classes
description: Bypass of CVE-2018-2628 fix using JDK classes for T3 protocol deserialization, enabling unauthenticated RCE.

name: Arbitrary file upload via web service test page
description: When the web service test page is enabled, an unauthenticated attacker can upload arbitrary files, including web shells.
recommendation: Apply Oracle CPU July 2018. Disable test pages in production.

name: wls-wsat OS command execution
description: The original wls-wsat vulnerability allows OS command execution via crafted SOAP XML requests.

name: XMLDecoder Deserialization bypass
description: Bypass of CVE-2019-2725 fix allows unauthenticated RCE via XMLDecoder deserialization in WebLogic.
recommendation: Apply Oracle out-of-band patch for CVE-2019-2729.

name: Local File Inclusion via path traversal
description: An unauthenticated attacker can read sensitive files from the WebLogic server via path traversal in the HTTP request.

name: IIOP Protocol RCE
description: Easily exploitable vulnerability via IIOP protocol allows unauthenticated attacker to compromise Oracle WebLogic Server.

name: T3 Protocol Deserialization RCE
description: Vulnerability in WebLogic Server Core Components via T3 protocol allows unauthenticated remote code execution.

name: IIOP/T3 Deserialization RCE
description: Vulnerability via T3 and IIOP protocols in WebLogic WLS Core allows unauthenticated remote code execution.

name: Arbitrary file upload via DeploymentService
description: Authenticated attacker can upload arbitrary files to the WebLogic server via the DeploymentService component.

name: IIOP/T3 Deserialization binary chain RCE
description: A new deserialization gadget chain combining binary and XML deserialization allows unauthenticated RCE via T3/IIOP.

name: T3/IIOP JNDI lookup injection
description: Unauthenticated attacker can exploit JNDI lookup via T3/IIOP to access or modify data accessible from WebLogic Server.

name: SSRF via UDDI Explorer
description: Server-side request forgery in the UDDI Explorer component allows an attacker to scan internal networks.
recommendation: Apply Oracle CPU July 2014. Remove or restrict UDDI Explorer.

name: T3 Deserialization RCE via StreamMessageImpl
description: T3 protocol deserialization using StreamMessageImpl as wrapper bypasses blacklist filtering, allowing unauthenticated RCE.

name: T3 Deserialization RCE via MarshalledObject
description: Uses MarshalledObject to wrap serialized gadgets, bypassing the blacklist and enabling RCE via T3.

name: T3/IIOP Remote Code Execution
description: Easily exploitable vulnerability allows unauthenticated attacker with network access via T3/IIOP to execute arbitrary code.

name: T3/IIOP Information Disclosure
description: Unauthenticated attacker with network access via T3/IIOP can access unauthorized data from Oracle WebLogic Server.

name: T3 Protocol Deserialization RCE
description: The T3 protocol deserialization in WebLogic Server WLS Core Components allows unauthenticated remote code execution.
